run_metadata.coverage_summary: Queried all nine Tier-1 government jurisdictions (AusTender, buy.nsw, Buying for Victoria, QTenders/supply.qld, Tenders WA, Tenders SA, Tenders Tasmania, NT QTOL, ACT Tenders) via web search; sampled Tier-2 (local councils via buy.nsw/tenders.sa.gov.au, universities UNSW and RMIT); and ran the mandatory Tier-3 corporate check (SAP Ariba Discovery / SAP Business Network). Captured five recently-published or open opportunities (NSW DPIRD Business Systems Assurance platform, Blacktown City Council tree-planting RFT, Copper Coast Council Jones Street Wallaroo reconstruction, UNSW physical blast-simulation facility RFT, RMIT Architecture Panel EOI) and one genuinely new buyer-originated source (RMIT Supply Portal / TenderMax at tendermax.rmit.edu.au). As in recent runs, direct page fetching was unavailable (WebFetch returned HTTP 403 to every external portal, including buy.nsw), so detail is limited to publicly-searchable headline level; no corporate RFx could be read behind registration walls.
gaps: Direct page retrieval (WebFetch) returned HTTP 403 Forbidden for every external site this run, including buy.nsw, tenders.gov.au, Tenders WA/SA/Tasmania, NT QTOL and ACT Tenders. Tender detail pages and specification documents could not be opened, so deliverables/requirements/evaluation-criteria extraction was not possible; all findings rely on public search snippets and must be treated as pending a detail pass.

Closing dates for the captured opportunities come from search-index and aggregator listings, not the buyer's own verified detail page. The NSW DPIRD Business Systems Assurance platform closing date was reported inconsistently across listings (21 July vs 24 July 2026) and is left null pending verification.

Tier-3 corporate opportunities (SAP Ariba Discovery / SAP Business Network) are login/registration-gated; no publicly-visible Australian corporate RFx was retrievable without a supplier account this run. The portal is recorded in discovered_sources with access_method=portal-registration; no corporate tenders were fabricated.
Invitation-only / panel-restricted RFx (e.g. management-consultancy RFPs sent to shortlisted firms) are out of scope and were not sought.

A Tasmanian DECYP opportunity ('Practical School Based Engagement and Attendance Program', DECYP_2398) surfaced but an aggregator listed it as already closed (15 July 2026); it was excluded rather than recorded as open.
Search index appears to lag by roughly 1-2 weeks; several returned listings had closing dates already passed relative to the run date and were excluded.
recommended_seed_additions: RMIT University Supply Portal (TenderMax) — https://tendermax.rmit.edu.au — buyer-originated university e-tendering portal (VIC) hosting EOIs/RFTs such as the RMIT Architecture Panel; distinct from RMIT's general procurement info page already known.
Copper Coast Council (SA) — publishes works quotations/tenders via SA Tenders and Contracts (tenders.sa.gov.au); a recurring local-government buyer worth monitoring.

tenders.title: Business Systems Assurance (BSA) Platform
tenders.buyer: NSW Department of Primary Industries and Regional Development (DPIRD)
tenders.description: DPIRD is seeking a Business Systems Assurance (BSA) Platform to provide a centralised assurance framework that demonstrates compliance, manages risk and establishes clear accountability across its business systems, supporting an automated Business Systems Assurance Framework spanning cyber, AI, data and accessibility domains.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Supply and implementation of a centralised Business Systems Assurance (GRC-type) platform enabling DPIRD to operate an automated assurance framework — formal policies, procedures and controls to protect digital assets, AI-enabled systems and IT infrastructure, with structured governance, risk management and compliance across cyber, AI, data and accessibility domains. (Summary drawn from the public opportunity description; full deliverables/scope not verified from the notice this run.)
tenders.reasoning: Discovered via web search of current buy.nsw opportunities; buy.nsw is the buyer-originated NSW Government procurement hub on which DPIRD posts. An aggregator listing (australiantenders.com.au, 'Governance, Risk and Compliance Solution') was used only as a corroborating discovery signal, not as the source of truth.
tenders.notes: Headline-level only. Closing date reported inconsistently across listings (21 July vs 24 July 2026) and left null pending verification; the buyer's own detail page (buy.nsw) returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool, so tender ID, exact dates, documents, evaluation criteria, value and contacts could not be confirmed. Deliverables_summary is paraphrased from the publicly-visible opportunity description, not from the tender documents.

tenders.title: Tree Planting, Preparation and Establishment Services
tenders.description: Blacktown City Council is seeking a contractor to provide tree planting, site preparation and establishment (maintenance) services, supporting the council's urban tree-planting and greening program.
tenders.reasoning: Discovered via web search of current buy.nsw tender opportunities; Blacktown City Council advertises via the buyer-originated buy.nsw hub.
tenders.notes: Headline-level only. Reference C16/2026 and closing 27 July 2026 15:00 are from a buy.nsw search listing; the detail page returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool so scope, documents, requirements, value and contacts could not be verified. Deliverables left blank rather than inferred.

tenders.title: Reconstruction of Jones Street, Wallaroo
tenders.description: Copper Coast Council is seeking quotations for the reconstruction of Jones Street, Wallaroo, SA, involving road reconstruction, stormwater installation, kerb demolition and related works.
tenders.submission_method: Lodge via SA Tenders and Contracts
tenders.reasoning: Discovered via web search; Copper Coast Council publishes works quotations/tenders through the buyer-originated SA Tenders and Contracts portal (tenders.sa.gov.au). Aggregator listings (australiantenders.com.au, govmarket.com.au) were used only as a discovery signal.
tenders.notes: Headline-level only. Closing 24 July 2026 is from an aggregator listing and could not be confirmed on the buyer's own portal (WebFetch HTTP 403). This is a companion project to a Moonta Road reconstruction by the same council captured previously; scope, value, documents and contacts remain unverified.

tenders.title: Supply of a National Facility for Physical Blast Simulation
tenders.description: UNSW is seeking suppliers for a national facility for physical blast simulation, i.e. specialised research infrastructure/equipment to physically simulate blast/explosive loading for structural and materials testing.
tenders.reasoning: Discovered via web search of Australian university procurement; UNSW runs its own buyer-originated e-tendering portal on TenderLink. An aggregator listing (australiantenders.com.au) was used only as a discovery signal, not as the source of truth.
tenders.notes: Headline-level only. Publish date 2026-07-16 is from an aggregator listing; the closing date, scope, documents, requirements and value could not be verified as the UNSW TenderLink detail page requires free registration and WebFetch returned HTTP 403 this run.

tenders.title: RMIT Architecture Panel — EOI for a panel of prequalified architects
tenders.description: RMIT University is seeking submissions to establish the RMIT Architecture Panel, prequalifying established and emerging architects/practices to provide architectural services for RMIT capital projects across one or more panel subcategories.
tenders.eligibility: Open to established and emerging architects/practices seeking prequalification for one or more panel subcategories.
tenders.reasoning: Discovered via web search of RMIT tenders; RMIT hosts its own buyer-originated tender portal at tendermax.rmit.edu.au (RMIT Supply Portal), where the Architecture Panel EOI documents are published. Aggregator listings were used only as a discovery signal.
tenders.notes: Headline-level only. Closing date and full requirements were not visible in search snippets; the RMIT TenderMax detail page requires supplier registration and WebFetch returned HTTP 403, so dates, evaluation criteria, documents and contacts remain unverified.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Found while searching RMIT University tenders (RMIT Architecture Panel EOI); RMIT publishes and receives tender submissions through its own buyer-originated portal at tendermax.rmit.edu.au, distinct from the general RMIT procurement information page already in the known-source list.
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated university e-tendering portal (not an aggregator). Full opportunity detail and documents require supplier registration.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Known buyer-originated university portal (added a prior run); re-checked this run and found a fresh RFT (National Facility for Physical Blast Simulation).
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated portal; full detail requires free supplier registration.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Canonical Tier-1 seed; checked this run via the search index. Direct fetch returned HTTP 403.
discovered_sources.notes: Central Commonwealth procurement portal. A new Supplier Portal rollout to all registered users is scheduled for July 2026. Individual ATM detail pages could not be fetched directly this run.

discovered_sources.notes: NSW Government Opportunities Hub; carries state agency and many council tenders.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Canonical Tier-1 seed; checked this run via search. No specific new open ACT opportunity surfaced in snippets this run.
Canonical Tier-2 seed; checked this run. Most councils publish here.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Mandatory Tier-3 check each run. Public posting board for corporate RFx; no publicly-visible Australian RFx was retrievable without a supplier account this run.
discovered_sources.notes: Largest single slice of corporate opportunity; full detail requires free supplier registration.
